channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Since Maduro’s Fall, Oil Is Flowing in Venezuela but Freedoms Are Not
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela — One-hundred-fourteen days after the capture of President Nicolás Maduro in a United States military attack, Venezuela is undergoing rapid change, but not all for the better. Citizens face continued financial hardships and repressive rights tactics carried out by the new government. The most visible changes have taken place in the lifting of ... Read more

item.title: No Kings? Meet King Don and King John Part 3 of 3
item.description: This is the third part of a three-part commentary. Read Part 1: No Kings? Meet King Don and King John – Part 1 of 3, Part 2 of 3 Whose head? In foreign relations, as in immigration, King Don the Con appears to be channeling King John the Bad and often surpassing him. However, our […]

item.title: Inside GEF-9: What it is and Why it Could Define the Next Four Years of Environmental Action
item.description: The Global Environment Facility’s new $3.9 billion funding cycle aims to accelerate environmental action by shifting from individual projects to system-wide environmental transformation.

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Fighting continues in Lebanon; Security Council to debate maritime security
item.description: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the UN Security Council is set to hold a high-level debate on maritime security amid ongoing concerns over the safety of key global waterways. Stay with us for live updates.

item.title: Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns
item.description: Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance – “an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community,” a senior official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF has said.

item.title: As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief
item.description: With top diplomats gathered at UN Headquarters to review the 1970 Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), the UN Secretary-General warned that it must evolve to survive the age of AI and other new technologies.

item.title: ‘Significant Stress’ as UN Prepares for Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Conference
item.description: The Eleventh Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) will meet at the United Nations in New York from 27 April to 22 May 2026. State parties to the treaty will meet with the urgent aim of finding common ground on the issue of nonproliferation. “The NPT […]

item.title: Indonesia’s Genocide Case Shines the Spotlight on Myanmar Atrocities
item.description: Yasmin Ullah, from Myanmar’s persecuted Rohingya minority, is determined to see justice. On 13 April, she filed a complaint alleging genocide against Myanmar’s president, Min Aung Hlaing, to Indonesia’s Attorney General’s Office. Min Aung Hlaing led the 2021 coup that ousted a democratically elected government and this month was named president following a sham election […]

item.title: Africa Faces Mounting Risks Just as Growth Gains Take Hold
item.description: Sub-Saharan Africa’s economies entered 2026 with significant momentum. The region had notched its fastest growth rate in 10 years—4.5 percent in 2025—buoyed by reduced macroeconomic imbalances, rising investment levels, and a generally supportive external environment. Countries such as Benin, Côte d’Ivoire, Ethiopia, and Rwanda led the charge, with growth exceeding 6 percent. The median inflation […]

item.title: From Struggle to Strength: Turning Daily Hustle Into a Force for Survival
item.description: In the bustling Chifubu constituency of Ndola, the provincial capital of Zambia’s mineral-rich Copperbelt Province, 31-year-old Victoria Bwalya is usually among the early risers, cleaning and setting up for the day in her restaurant business. But before now, Bwalya’s hustle felt like a punishment and just a matter of survival. With only a primary school […]
